title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs clock, delta time, fixed steps, interpolation, and stalls from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded deterministic time-step policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 9299 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

Inspect the Time Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Time Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Time Evidence, keep Time Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

The record names the assumption, applies Time Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Time Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 2632 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Time Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Time Evidence, keep Time Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.
